items.caption_text:
When Parul Gulati and Karan Aujla started trending, we moved fast. 

No overthinking, no long approvals, just pure moment marketing.

Because in today’s world, relevance beats perfection.
If you can ride the moment right, the internet does the distribution for you.

items.caption_text:
The restaurant business looks glamorous from the outside, but behind the scenes it’s one of the toughest games to win.

Most people enter, very few survive, and even fewer scale.

In this episode, we decode how Abhijit Gupta built not just one, but multiple successful brands like The Pizza Bakery, Paris Panini and Smash Guys.

From product to positioning to execution, this is a masterclass on what actually works in the restaurant business.

Full episode releasing soon.

Full episode releasing soon.

items.caption_text:
Passion feels powerful, but it’s not a strategy.

A lot of creators start with pure passion and burn out when it doesn’t convert into growth. Not because they lack talent, but because they lack direction.

In today’s world, content is not just expression. It’s positioning.

You need to ask
Who is this for
Why should they care
What value does this create

Passion will get you started
Strategy will keep you relevant

The creators who win are not just passionate
They are intentional

Build with passion
But grow with strategy

items.caption_text:
You don’t need a niche to start, you need a voice.

Yes, having a niche helps. It gives direction. But if you don’t have that clarity yet, don’t wait. Start with your personality.

Because at the end of the day, people don’t follow niches, they follow people. And real personality always wins.

Be raw. Be honest. Be you. 
That’s your biggest advantage.

Be raw. Be honest. Be you.

items.caption_text:
HYROX isn’t just another race.
It’s a full blown reality check.

As a founder, you deal with pressure, uncertainty, and constant problem solving every day. But this hits differently.

No team to rely on. No excuses. No shortcuts. Just you, your discipline, and how far your mind can really push your body.

Somewhere between the fatigue and the finish line, you meet a different version of yourself. The one that does not quit.

At least once in your life, you should experience this.

Because HYROX will not just test your fitness. It will expose your mindset.

items.caption_text:
We live in a world where content is no longer scarce.

Everyone is creating, posting, building.

But the real gap? Distribution.
Because it’s not about who creates the best - 
it’s about who gets seen the most.

items.caption_text:
We didn’t start LIT School  to follow the traditional way of learning.

We started it because we saw a gap between what people are taught and what actually works in the real world.

This is a space where creators don’t just learn, they build, experiment, fail, and grow.

We’re not here to give degrees. 

We’re here to create people who don’t need one.

items.caption_text:
Your work deserves more than just being good. It deserves to be seen.

Because in today’s world, it’s not the best work that wins.

It’s the best known work that wins.
Put yourself out there. Show up. Be visible.

Attend events, meet people, build real connections. This is where opportunities actually begin.
That’s why platforms like startup awards and industry events matter.

They don’t just recognize you. They amplify you.

If you’re building something, don’t stay in the shadows.
Step into the room where visibility meets opportunity.

Apply now 🚀

items.caption_text:
When I started, I wanted to build for everyone.
Bigger vision, bigger market, bigger impact.

Reality check, that’s the fastest way to go nowhere.

The moment I narrowed down and picked a niche, everything changed.
Clear messaging. Better product decisions. Real traction.

You don’t start big. You earn the right to go big.

Start with a niche. Win there.
Then expand.

You don’t start big. You earn the right to go big.

Start with a niche. Win there.
Then expand.

items.caption_text:
Most people want to start big. 
As a founder, that’s the fastest way to stay invisible.

When I started, I didn’t chase everyone. I chose a niche. That’s where the first traction came from. That’s where people started recognizing the work, trusting the process, and coming back for more.

A niche isn’t limiting, it’s leverage.
 It sharpens your message, builds authority faster, and gives your brand a clear identity.

Start small.
 Own it completely. Then expand.

items.caption_text:
Everyone is busy creating content.
But very few are building distribution.

Content is no longer the advantage. It’s the entry ticket.

Distribution is the leverage.

The one who knows how to place the content in front of the right people wins.

In today’s game, it’s not about who creates the best.
It’s about who gets seen the most.

items.caption_text:
Every platform is a different battlefield.

What works on Instagram won’t work on YouTube.
What converts on LinkedIn might fail on Twitter.
As a brand, if you treat every platform the same, you’re already losing.

Understand the language of each platform.
Play to its strength.

Market with intent, not repetition.

That’s when growth stops being random and starts becoming predictable.

items.caption_text:
The genius of Indian Premier League isn’t just cricket… it’s distribution.

They don’t just host matches, they sell attention.
Broadcast rights → billions
Streaming rights → billions

Sponsorships → everywhere you look
Every six, every wicket, every timeout is already monetized before the game even begins.
It’s not a sports league.

It’s a media empire disguised as entertainment.

items.caption_text:
In 2019, I was on a ventilator.

Thin kid. Junk food every day. Never thought twice about my health. Until my body completely shut down on me.
Woke up and the only thought was. Never again.

Started running. Started eating right. First real win: crossing the finish line of a 21km half marathon.
This weekend, my best friend and I ran Hyrox. Four hours of sleep, zero race-specific prep, and absolutely no regrets.
We crossed the finish line in 1 hour 40 minutes.

As a founder, the conversation is always about output, performance, execution.
Nobody talks about the machine behind all of it.

What helped us stay sharp through the race was keeping an eye on our vitals with Valour 1R- that made me realize the importance of tracking your progress and realizing your truest potential.

Super happy to be a part of the Valour athlete family!

#ValourAthlete

items.caption_text:
Most brands forget this one rule. Attribution is the biggest problem in influencer marketing.

As a founder, I have realized this the hard way. You can get views, engagement, even sales, but if you cannot clearly track where it’s coming from, you are just guessing.

Influencer marketing is not broken, your measurement is. If you are not solving attribution, you are not scaling, you are just spending.

raw.caption:

Influencer marketing is not broken, your measurement is. If you are not solving attribution, you are not scaling, you are just spending.

items.caption_text:
When you grow from 5 clients to 80+, you can’t track every conversation. Most founders start managing based on "vibes" or whichever fire is burning the loudest. But vibes aren’t a strategy.

I’ve shifted my philosophy from micro-managing to macro-auditing.

Instead of scrolling through endless chats, I now use @chatgptindia as my objective lens. It filters through the noise to find the patterns and gaps that the human eye misses when it’s too close to the work.

When you use AI to remove bias from your reviews, you stop managing through ego and start managing through data.

#chatgpt

items.caption_text:
Before you invest, do this first.

Most people lose money not because the idea was bad, but because they never understood the market.
Start with demand. Are people actively looking for this or are you forcing it? 

Study competitors. What are they doing right and where are they lacking? Talk to real users. Not your friends. Not your assumptions. Actual buyers. Check pricing. If people are not willing to pay, you don’t have a business. 
And most importantly, validate small before you scale big.

Market research is not a step. It is the difference between guessing and building something that actually works.

raw.caption:

Start with demand. Are people actively looking for this or are you forcing it? 

Study competitors. What are they doing right and where are they lacking? Talk to real users. Not your friends. Not your assumptions. Actual buyers. Check pricing. If people are not willing to pay, you don’t have a business. 
And most importantly, validate small before you scale big.

Market research is not a step. It is the difference between guessing and building something that actually works.

items.caption_text:
As a brand, you need to understand how to use different social media platforms.

Because every platform plays a different game.
What works on Instagram won’t work on YouTube.
What works on LinkedIn won’t work on Twitter.

Audience behavior changes. Content format changes. Attention span changes.

The brands that win aren’t everywhere
they’re relevant everywhere.
So don’t just post.

Adapt, position, and communicate based on the platform.

That’s how you actually grow.

That’s how you actually grow.

Saved 10 Lakhs this year and here's the secret no one talks about...

Every business needs systems. But not every person in your business needs the same system.

Your designer doesn't need what your accountant needs.

Your developer doesn't need what your sales team needs.

So why are you buying one-size-fits-all?

This year, I switched to LenovoPro and the game changed completely.

Fully customizable systems for every role in my team

A dedicated Account Manager who actually gets your business

Smarter spending = 10 Lakhs back in my pocket Stop overpaying for tech that doesn't fit.

Build smarter. Scale faster.

Comment "TECH" and I'll drop the exact checklist we use to buy equipment.

#Lenovo #SmarterTechnology #lenovopro

items.caption_text:
If you want to create content in 2026,
you need to be passionate about what you’re putting out.

Because trends will fade. Algorithms will change.
But passion is what keeps you consistent when nothing is working.

It’s what makes you show up on bad days.
It’s what separates creators who quit from creators who grow.

Anyone can start.

But only the ones who genuinely care will last.
So don’t just create content.
Create something you actually believe in

items.caption_text:
Content creation has 3 waves.

First, passion. You create because you love it.
Second, FOMO. You see others winning and you jump in.

But the third wave, that’s where things get serious.
Strategy.
In 2026, you don’t need to wait for passion to magically appear.

You can start with clarity, systems, and intent.
Because creators who win today aren’t just passionate
they’re calculated.

Start smart. Passion can follow

items.caption_text:
Success is rarely absolute.
It is always measured against a reference point.

If your reference point is comfort, small wins will feel like success.
If your reference point is survival, stability will feel like success.

But if your reference point is excellence, impact, and building something that outlives you… the bar automatically rises.

As founders, the biggest responsibility we have is not just building a business. It is choosing the reference point we measure our life against.

Set your bar where most people feel uncomfortable.
Set your bar where mediocrity cannot survive.
Set your bar where growth becomes the only option.

Because the higher your reference point, the higher your version of success becomes.

As founders, the biggest responsibility we have is not just building a business. It is choosing the reference point we measure our life against.

Set your bar where most people feel uncomfortable.
Set your bar where mediocrity cannot survive.
Set your bar where growth becomes the only option.

Because the higher your reference point, the higher your version of success becomes.

items.caption_text:
When you become a manager, you stop practicing your skills and that’s a very big mistake.

Somewhere between meetings, deadlines, and managing people, you start believing your past expertise is enough. 

But the truth is, skills don’t stay where you left them. They either grow or they fade.
As a founder or leader, your role isn’t just to manage, it’s to stay sharp. Because the moment you disconnect from the craft, you lose your edge, your instincts, and eventually, your relevance.

The best leaders I’ve seen are still in the trenches. Still learning. Still practicing.

So no matter how senior you get, never stop doing the work.

Because leadership without skill is just noise

The best leaders I’ve seen are still in the trenches. Still learning. Still practicing.

So no matter how senior you get, never stop doing the work.

Because leadership without skill is just noise

items.caption_text:
Working with Aman Gupta is a different game altogether.

Back-to-back shoots, long hours, zero breaks… yet his energy never drops.

While most people slow down, he shows up sharper, louder, and more present every single time.
As a founder, you realize it’s not just about building a company - it’s about building stamina, mindset, and an obsession to keep going when others tap out.
That level of consistency?

That’s what truly separates the good from the great.

items.caption_text:
7 months ago, I randomly came across this tool while watching a Steven Bartlett podcast.

 He recommended it, I downloaded it the same day. Within a week I bought the paid subscription. No hesitation.

And honestly? It's one of the best decisions I've made for my productivity.

It's not a fancy AI tool. It doesn't do 100 things. It does one thing, voice to text. But it does it so insanely well that it has completely changed the way I work.

Every WhatsApp reply. Every email. Every Slack message. Every G-Chat. I just speak now. I don't type anymore.

It catches names correctly. It structures messages properly. It removes filler words. It just works across every single app on my phone and laptop.
And the time savings? I'm saving roughly 30 to 35 minutes every single day. That's 3+ hours a week. Just from not typing.

We never realize how much time we waste typing until something takes it away from you.

Apart from ChatGPT and Claude, if there's one tool worth paying for, this is it. Every working professional needs this in their life.

@wisprflow . Speak to your devices. Get everything done.
#ad

items.caption_text:
Obsession gets you started. Perspective takes you further.

I'm obsessive about what I build and I wear that proudly.

But here's what I've learned: my best ideas never came from a meeting room or a shoot. They came from a hotel lobby, a trip, a random Tuesday doing something completely unrelated.

The principle that defines everything I create? It came from the hospitality industry, simply because I chose to stay curious beyond my craft.
Obsession is the fuel. But a wider lens is the engine.
Because what gets you here won't always get you there.

Stay obsessed. But stay open

Because what gets you here won't always get you there.

Stay obsessed. But stay open

items.caption_text:
One email. That’s all it takes. @owledmedia has seen a massive surge of demand because of emailing right.

Not 100 connections. Not years of waiting.

Just one bold message sent at the right time to the right person.

People underestimate cold emails because they’re scared of being ignored.

But they forget - life-changing opportunities can also start with strangers.

Your next big break might be sitting in your drafts right now.

Send it.
